title: Appeals to NT Government to reject fracking near hot springs | ABC NEWS
description: Water scientists are worried 12 test fracking wells on a remote cattle station could damage a springs system that is still little-known and understood.  Indigenous custodians are worried that the Santos test fracking project being considered by the NT government could affect wildlife and songlines.  Santos has said it will use strict environmental controls to protect aquifers and rivers, and put a water monitoring program in place to detect any problems.   Jane Bardon reports for ABC News Northern Territory.   ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: SASES trial drones to assist stranded motorists in flood emergencies | ABC NEWS
description: South Australia's SES has used a talking drone that can get up close to drivers' windows and communicate with motorists stranded by floodwater. It said it trialled the technology for the first time during an extreme weather event in early March, when record rainfall lashed parts of the state, isolating some communities. title: Iranians share their experiences about the US-Israel war in Iran | ABC NEWS
description: 📍 The ABC heard from many Iranians inside the country in the hours after Israel and the United States began their strikes.  Most communications took place via voice notes during intermittent periods of internet connection, because of the near-total phone and internet blackout imposed by the regime since early January.  People inside Iran can face retaliation from the regime if they talk to foreign media, so most names in this video are pseudonyms to protect their identities.  📸 via ABC News by reporter Nassim Khadem.   ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. It's news when you want it, from Australia's most trusted news organisation. title: Katherine River reaches major flood level with emergency warning in place | ABC NEWS

title: Iran's drones wreak havoc across Middle East | ABC NEWS
description: One-way Iranian attack drones — often referred to as suicide or kamikaze drones — have reshaped warfare, with Russia using them to unleash relentless, deadly barrages against Ukrainian cities.  Unlike traditional drones that fly on a mission and return home, the drones were designed as ammunition, diving into targets with an explosive warhead.  Now Iran is firing them in large numbers across the Arab Gulf, retaliating against the US and Israel's massive and ongoing attacks.  Includes reporting by Annika Burgess, Reuters, AP. Social video by David Douglas Stuart for the ABC News Social Media Team.   ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad.
